assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all four notification types with specific sections for security patches, end-of-life warnings, auto-upgrade notifications, and available version updates

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response uses color-coded priority system with security patches marked as 'CRITICAL' (red), auto-upgrades as 'HIGH' (yellow), new version available as 'MEDIUM' (green), and general announcements as 'LOW' (blue)

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response specifically addresses end-of-life warnings with different urgency levels based on timeline (<30 days = critical immediate upgrade, 30-90 days = plan upgrade timeline) and emphasizes loss of support

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response provides a detailed 'Response Playbook' section with specific commands for assessing impact, checking current version, and planning upgrades for auto-upgrade notifications

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response marks security patches as 'CRITICAL - Immediate Action Required (0-7 days)' and explicitly states they can be exploited, implying urgency beyond normal maintenance schedules

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response categorizes 'Available Version Updates' as 'MEDIUM - Plan Ahead (1-3 months)' with action to 'Evaluate for next maintenance window' and states 'Stay current but not urgent'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts specifically. It covers email filtering, Slack integration, and basic monitoring but lacks the specific GCP services mentioned in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE release notes as a source for understanding version changes. It focuses on notification triage and response procedures but doesn't reference release notes for change details
